name: Muel Waterfalls and San Borombón Hill - circular from Muel
name_original: Cascadas de Muel y Cabezo de San Borombón — circular desde Muel
region_intro: Hiking around Muel offers diverse landscapes, characterized by its unique geological formations and the presence of significant water features. The region features a mix of rolling hills, river valleys, and the notable Muel Waterfalls. Trails often traverse varied terrain, including areas around historical sites like the Roman Dam. This area provides opportunities for exploring both natural beauty and cultural heritage on foot. Best hiking trails around Muel The most popular hiking route is Muel Waterfalls and San Borombón Hill - circular from Muel, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the Muel Waterfalls and ascends San Borombón Hill. Another top favourite among local hikers is María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.1 km) path. This longer route offers expansive views and a more challenging experience through the landscape. Local hikers also love the María de Huerva Castle – Collado Sillón Cairn loop from María de Huerva, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Muel is defined by waterfalls, hills, and historical dam sites. The network offers options for easy to moderate trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Muel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Muel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Muel?
A: Yes, Muel offers several easy hiking trails. There are 5 routes classified as easy, perfect for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfall – Muel Waterfalls loop from Arañales de Muel&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.3 miles (5.4 km) long and takes around 1 hour 25 minutes to complete.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hikes around Muel?
A: Hikes in Muel vary in length and duration. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfall – Muel Waterfalls loop&lt;/a&gt; at 3.3 miles (5.4 km) taking about 1 hour 25 minutes, up to longer, more challenging paths such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974956789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.7 miles (22.1 km) and can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Muel?
A: Yes, Muel features several circular routes, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976041904/muel-waterfalls-and-san-borombon-hill-circular-from-muel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls and San Borombón Hill - circular from Muel&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that explores the waterfalls and ascends San Borombón Hill.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Muel?
A: The hiking trails in Muel are known for their unique geological formations and water features. You can explore the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and ascend to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3135935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de San Borombón&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1636318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Sillón Cairn&lt;/a&gt; and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3135934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivy Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to discover while hiking in Muel?
A: Absolutely. Muel's trails often traverse areas with cultural heritage. A significant historical site you can encounter is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Muel&lt;/a&gt;. You might also come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de Dorleta Memorial&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Muel?
A: Muel offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the varied terrain. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is also possible, but be prepared for cooler conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Muel?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on the hiking trails in Muel. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers, and to clean up after them. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for the trails in this region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Muel?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Muel, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the presence of the Muel Waterfalls, and the mix of natural beauty with historical sites like the Roman Dam.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes in Muel?
A: Yes, Muel has a good selection of moderate hikes, with 8 routes falling into this category. These trails offer a bit more challenge than easy routes but are still accessible to most hikers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls – Roman Dam of Muel loop from Arañales de Muel&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) route.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Muel?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trailhead, Muel is generally accessible, and you can typically find parking near the starting points of the main hiking routes, especially in or around the village of Muel or other nearby towns like Arañales de Muel.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails in Muel?
A: Muel is a small town, and you can usually find local establishments like cafes or restaurants within the village itself. It's a good idea to check opening times, especially if you plan to hike early or late. For trails starting further afield, it's best to pack snacks and drinks.
tips: In the surroundings of Zaragoza there are many great little excursions, and one of the most complete, since it brings together nature, history and culture in one place, is a visit to the Muel Natural Park.
The Muel Roman dam is one of the most relevant hydraulic civil engineering works in the Hispano-Roman world, due to its monumentality and good state of conservation.

The Cabezo San Borombón is a place of great interest in the town of Muel. Next Sunday, July 14, 2024, the 12th edition of the Trail Muel: Cabezo de San Borombón will be held in Muel.
In summary, Cabezo San Borombón and its surroundings offer a unique combination of nature, history and sport. An experience worth living! 🏃‍♂️🏞️🌟
